commands.review_summary:
Refresh Gemini web and API model mappings, centralize current-model resolution and legacy aliases, enable current Gemini browser routes, and update documentation and regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main contains stale private model headers and rejects Gemini 3.1 Pro browser routing, while the PR body gives a concrete authenticated 120-second main failure and successful branch timings. This review did not independently rerun the signed-in browser path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Because this is an owner-authored provider-routing change, the remaining action is explicit maintainer acceptance of the documented private-header and account-entitlement risk rather than an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(gemini): refresh web model mappings
target.body:
## Summary

- refresh Gemini web private model headers for Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Pro Deep Think
- centralize current web model resolution, legacy aliases, and fallback selection
- add current Gemini Flash API model configs and allow Gemini 3.1 Pro in browser mode
- update CLI help, docs, changelog, unit/integration coverage, and live regression coverage

Fixes #242.

## Product decision

Oracle keeps explicit model IDs. It does not add generic `low`, `medium`, or `high` aliases because those names obscure exact model/billing identity and overlap with thinking-effort semantics.

Current explicit IDs:

- `gemini-3.1-flash-lite`
- `gemini-3.5-flash`
- `gemini-3.1-pro`

Legacy Gemini web names remain accepted at the resolver boundary. `gemini-3-pro` browser runs now target current Gemini 3.1 Pro; old 2.5 browser names map to the current Pro or Flash-Lite replacement.

Official references:

- https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models/gemini-3.5-flash
- https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models/gemini-3.1-flash-lite
- https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models

## Reproduction and live proof

On unmodified `main`, the focused authenticated cookie-client text smoke using the stale Gemini 3 Pro header timed out at 120 seconds. The same signed-in Gemini account answered current UI model probes in seconds.

After this patch, the normal source executor passed against the existing authenticated Chrome profile:

- Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite: 2.56 seconds
- Gemini 3.5 Flash: 2.69 seconds
- Gemini 3.1 Pro: 14.78 seconds

Each returned a valid short response through Oracle's direct Gemini cookie client. Live inspection also confirmed the current picker labels and captured the model-header shapes used by the deterministic regression tests. Account identity, cookies, and generated client identifiers were not logged.

## Validation

- `ORACLE_LIVE_TEST=1 pnpm exec vitest run tests/live/gemini-web-live.test.ts -t 'returns a short text answer' --reporter=verbose` (3 passed)
- `pnpm test` (1,233 passed, 43 skipped)
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm docs:check`
- `pnpm docs:site`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test:packed-cli`
- `pnpm test:mcp`
- `$autoreview --mode local`: clean
- `$autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
